_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that verbs do not just describe actions — they also describe feelings, thoughts, speech and relationships between things.

_source.w: We are learning that verbs do not just describe actions — they also describe feelings, thoughts, speech and relationships between things.
y: Understanding different types of verbs helps us write more precisely and expressively.
This expands the range of things we can describe in our writing.
Knowing verb types helps us analyse how authors create meaning.
eg: We sort verbs into groups: doing (run, build), feeling (love, fear), thinking (believe, wonder), saying (whispered, shouted) and relating (is, has).
We find examples of each type of verb in our reading.
We use different verb types in our own writing to add variety.
We discuss why an author chose a 'saying' verb like 'whispered' instead of 'said'.
We rewrite sentences using different verb types and compare the effect.
We create a verb wall in our classroom organised by process type.
